response.world_assessment: Quiet holiday week with limited market-moving news. Key signal: ASML's China export license partially revoked by Dutch government, reinforcing the semiconductor decoupling trend and potentially benefiting US-based AI chip companies. US consumer debt approaching pre-COVID levels suggests consumer stress building, which could pressure discretionary spending. Political uncertainty around 2024 election cycle is noise for now. Overall, no major regime change — the AI infrastructure buildout and GLP-1 healthcare revolution remain the dominant investment themes entering 2024.

new_positions.thesis: NVIDIA is the dominant supplier of AI training and inference chips. The AI infrastructure buildout is still in early innings — hyperscalers (MSFT, GOOG, AMZN, META) are all accelerating capex on AI compute. ASML's China export restrictions further entrench NVIDIA's competitive position by limiting Chinese alternatives. Data center revenue is inflecting dramatically and the software moat (CUDA ecosystem) creates high switching costs.
new_positions.invalidation: 1) AI capex spending by hyperscalers declines materially, 2) AMD or custom silicon (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium) takes >20% market share in training, 3) Revenue growth decelerates below 30% YoY for two consecutive quarters
new_positions.timing_note: RSI at 50, neutral territory — not chasing a spike. Above SMA50 confirms uptrend intact. MACD bearish suggests possible short-term dip which would be welcome for averaging in.

new_positions.thesis: Eli Lilly is at the center of the GLP-1 revolution with Mounjaro (diabetes) and Zepbound (obesity). The obesity drug market is projected to reach $100B+ and LLY has best-in-class efficacy data. This intersects both the aging population theme and a massive untapped market — over 40% of US adults are obese. Pipeline beyond GLP-1 includes Alzheimer's (donanemab) and other aging-related conditions. Supply constraints are the only real limiter, and LLY is investing heavily in manufacturing.
new_positions.invalidation: 1) Competitor GLP-1 drugs show superior efficacy or safety profile, 2) Serious safety signal emerges from long-term GLP-1 use, 3) Pricing pressure from regulation cuts revenue per patient by >30%, 4) Donanemab FDA rejection without path forward
new_positions.timing_note: RSI at 56, MACD bullish — constructive setup. Above SMA50. Not bargain-basement but the secular trend justifies building a position now rather than waiting for a dip that may not come.

new_positions.thesis: Costco benefits from the wealth inequality theme — as consumer debt rises toward pre-COVID levels and middle-class budgets tighten, value-oriented retailers gain share. COST's membership model creates recurring revenue with 90%+ renewal rates. The company consistently takes market share in groceries, gas, and general merchandise. Defensive growth profile with pricing power through private label (Kirkland).
new_positions.invalidation: 1) Membership renewal rate drops below 88%, 2) Same-store sales go negative for two consecutive quarters, 3) Margin compression from wage inflation without offsetting membership fee increases
new_positions.timing_note: RSI at 58 with bearish MACD — not ideal timing. Starting with smaller 5% position and willing to add on a pullback toward SMA50. Patience here.

lessons: Starting a fresh portfolio during a holiday week with thin liquidity — sizing conservatively (19% total) and keeping 81% cash to deploy on better setups. No need to rush to fill the portfolio.
response.weekly_summary: Initiated three core positions (NVDA 7%, LLY 7%, COST 5%) representing our highest-conviction themes: AI infrastructure buildout, GLP-1/aging population healthcare revolution, and value retail amid rising consumer stress. Kept 81% cash deliberately — the market rallied hard in Q4 2023 and we want dry powder for pullbacks. ASML China restrictions reinforce the semiconductor thesis. No compelling short candidates this week. Will look to add climate transition and financial services exposure on better technical setups.

response.world_assessment: CES 2024 is dominating this week and is a major catalyst for NVDA. Jensen Huang showcased new RTX 40 Super GPUs, automotive partnerships (Li Auto choosing DRIVE Thor), and Amgen building drug discovery on NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD. The AI infrastructure buildout narrative is accelerating — our core thesis is playing out in real time. Boeing 737 Max 9 door plug incident is a negative for aerospace but not portfolio-relevant. BYD overtaking Tesla in global EV sales is a notable secular shift but we have no EV exposure. Macro environment remains benign with markets near highs into the new year.

thesis_updates.notes: Thesis STRENGTHENING. CES 2024 delivered multiple catalysts: new RTX Super GPUs, Li Auto selecting DRIVE Thor for next-gen fleets, Amgen partnering on DGX SuperPOD for drug discovery AI. Stock hit all-time highs. The breadth of announcements — gaming, automotive, pharma AI — confirms NVIDIA is not a one-trick pony but the platform layer for AI across industries. RSI 69 is warm but not overbought; momentum justified by fundamentals. Up ~10% from entry. Holding.

No material news this week. Thesis intact — GLP-1 demand remains strong and obesity market TAM unchanged. Up ~5.6% from entry. RSI 69 with bullish MACD. Quiet weeks are fine for a 6-12 month hold. Next catalyst will be Q4 earnings and any Zepbound supply/demand updates.

Unusual options activity noted — worth monitoring but not actionable alone. MACD turning bearish is a mild caution flag. Up ~2.5% from entry. Thesis on consumer value-seeking behavior remains intact. No fundamental change. Holding.

lessons: CES proved to be a strong catalyst week for semiconductor/AI names — in future years, consider building positions in AI-adjacent names ahead of CES (late December) when RSI is favorable, as the event consistently generates multiple positive news cycles.
response.weekly_summary: Week 2 of portfolio operation. Quiet macro environment with CES 2024 as the main event. NVDA thesis significantly strengthened by breadth of CES announcements spanning gaming, automotive, and pharma AI. All three positions in green (NVDA +10%, LLY +5.6%, COST +2.5%). No compelling new entries from emerging opportunities — HMC/INTC at CES lack strong thesis alignment, PRCT too early to evaluate. Maintaining conservative 82% cash position. No urgency to deploy capital; waiting for pullbacks or high-conviction setups.

response.world_assessment: Fed Governor Waller's comments this week are the dominant macro signal — the Fed is in no rush to cut rates, pushing back on the aggressive rate cut expectations that fueled the late-2023 rally. Treasury yields and the dollar are rising in response. This creates a near-term headwind for growth/momentum names but doesn't change our structural theses. The market is digesting post-CES enthusiasm and recalibrating rate expectations. No recession consensus for 2024 among institutional investors, which supports our long bias.

thesis_updates.notes: Up ~16% from entry at $560.53. RSI at 74 is overbought and price is near upper Bollinger Band — short-term pullback risk is elevated. However, post-CES analyst optimism is strong, institutional investors are long, and the AI infrastructure buildout thesis is only strengthening. Insider selling is a mild yellow flag but common after big runs. Trending on WSB adds noise but doesn't change fundamentals. Thesis intact — hold but not adding here.

Price at $628.91, up ~6% from entry. Lilly's international head explicitly confirmed they're building as much tirzepatide capacity as possible — directly validates our supply-constraint thesis. Mounjaro expansion depends on regulatory approvals and sufficient supply, both of which are progressing. Options activity suggests institutional interest. MACD bullish, RSI 63 is healthy. Thesis strengthening.

Price at $684.19, up ~5% from entry. Smart money active in COST options per news flow. MACD turning bearish is a near-term timing concern but RSI at 69 isn't extreme. The value-retail thesis remains intact — consumer debt trends and budget pressures haven't changed. Hold.

lessons: When the Fed pushes back on rate cut expectations (as Waller did this week), watch for rotation out of momentum/growth into value — this can create entry opportunities in quality names that pull back on sentiment rather than fundamentals. Flag future Fed speaker events as potential volatility catalysts.
response.weekly_summary: Week of Jan 12-17: Fed Gov. Waller tempered rate cut expectations, lifting yields and the dollar. All three positions are in profit — NVDA +16% (overbought near-term), LLY +6% (thesis strengthening on capacity build comments), COST +5% (smart money active). No new positions opened — emerging opportunities (EOLS, NNBR, PRGS) don't align with our core themes. Maintaining 82% cash and waiting for better entries, particularly if the Waller hawkishness creates a broader pullback.

response.world_assessment: Quiet week macro-wise. Semiconductor bullishness continues with multiple analysts calling for double-digit sector gains in 2024, driven by AI capex. Cathie Wood expects rates to fall sooner and faster than consensus — if true, this is bullish for growth/tech. No major geopolitical disruptions. Russia-India BRICS digital economy collaboration is worth monitoring long-term but no immediate portfolio impact. Overall environment remains constructive for our AI and healthcare themes.

thesis_updates.notes: Thesis strongly confirmed — multiple analyst reports this week calling NVDA the leader of a 2024 semiconductor rally. 'Data as the new oil' narrative gaining traction. Allxon collaboration on industrial edge AI shows expanding TAM beyond data centers. However, RSI at 84 is extremely overbought and price ($613.62) is within 1% of our $620 target. The thesis is intact but the easy money has been made on this leg up. Should reduce and look to re-enter on a pullback.

Thesis intact. Cantor Fitzgerald reiterates Overweight with $630 target — we're at $633.70, already exceeding their target. Whale alerts in healthcare session suggest institutional interest. Gene therapy pipeline (Akouos subsidiary) adds optionality beyond GLP-1. RSI at 64 is healthy, not overextended. Hold full position — our $720 target has room to run.

No specific news this week. MACD turning bearish is a minor technical concern but RSI at 66 is fine. Price at $686.51 trending toward our $740 target. Wealth inequality thesis unchanged — consumer credit stress will continue to drive value-seeking behavior. Hold.

reduce_positions.reason: RSI at 84 is extremely overbought and price is within 1% of our $620 target. Thesis remains strong but risk/reward has deteriorated at these levels. Reducing from ~8.6% to 5% to lock in gains. Will look to add back on any pullback toward $540-560 range where RSI resets below 50.

lessons: When a position reaches its target price AND RSI exceeds 80, take partial profits even if the thesis is intact. The thesis can remain valid while the entry point becomes poor — separate conviction in the story from conviction in the price. Flag any position approaching target with RSI > 75 for review.
response.weekly_summary: Week 4 of 2024: Constructive week for our portfolio. All three positions in the green. NVDA approaching target at $613.62 with RSI extremely overbought at 84 — reducing allocation from ~8.6% to 5% to lock in ~27% gain on partial position. LLY steady at $633.70 with continued institutional interest and pipeline catalysts. COST grinding higher at $686.51. No compelling new entries from emerging opportunities this week — SAP earnings were strong but doesn't fit our themes cleanly. Maintaining high cash position (~85% after NVDA trim) for better entries. Patient approach paying off.

response.world_assessment: Quiet macro week. IMF affirms AI growth and lower rates are offsetting recession risk — supportive of our positioning. NVDA continues to dominate AI narrative (securing HBM for 2025, investing in AI startups, top trending on WSB). Medicare drug price negotiation headlines are a long-term watch for LLY but unlikely to impact GLP-1 drugs near-term. Tech concentration warnings (dot-com comparisons) are noise for now but worth monitoring if breadth narrows further. Treasury funding optimism ahead of refunding announcement is mildly positive for risk assets.

thesis_updates.notes: Price $615.27 now within 1% of $620 target. RSI 74 — approaching Lesson 4 trigger (RSI > 75 near target). Thesis is actually strengthening: secured HBM for 2025 (supply advantage), investing in AI ecosystem (Kore.ai), IMF endorsing AI growth narrative. Raising target to $680 given accelerating thesis. Will take partial profits if RSI exceeds 80.

Price $645.61, up 9% from entry. RSI 67, healthy. Medicare price negotiation headlines are a background risk but GLP-1 drugs are too new to be in the first negotiation cohort. Thesis intact — obesity market still massively underpenetrated.
Price $694.88, up 7% from entry. MACD turning bearish is a timing concern but fundamentals unchanged. Options activity noted in news — watching for institutional positioning signals. Hold.

lessons: When a position approaches its original target but the underlying thesis has materially strengthened (new catalysts, expanding TAM, improving competitive position), raise the target rather than mechanically selling. The target should reflect the thesis strength, not just the original estimate. Apply Lesson 4 (partial profit-taking at target + RSI > 80) to the revised target.
response.weekly_summary: Week 5: Quiet consolidation week. All three positions performing well — NVDA +28% approaching original target (raised to $680 on strengthening AI buildout thesis), LLY +9% (GLP-1 thesis intact despite Medicare negotiation noise), COST +7% (holding steady). Portfolio value $101.8K, 84% cash. No new positions opened — emerging opportunities (CB, TER) don't align strongly enough with our themes. Maintaining patient posture with significant dry powder for better setups.

response.world_assessment: Quiet macro week with no major headline shifts. The AI/tech trade remains strong but extended — NVDA and LLY both blew through original targets with high RSI readings. Notable: multiple analyst upgrades on LLY (Wells Fargo PT $825, Cantor PT $815) confirm the GLP-1 thesis is strengthening. Josh Brown publicly trimming NVDA is a sentiment signal that some smart money is taking profits at these levels. No Fed speakers creating volatility this week. Market remains in a risk-on posture but getting stretched technically.

thesis_updates.notes: Price $700.99 has far exceeded $620 target (+45% from entry). RSI=82 signals overbought. Josh Brown trimming publicly. However, AI infrastructure thesis is STRONGER — hyperscaler capex commitments continue to accelerate. Per Lesson 5, raising target to $800. Per Lesson 4, recommending partial profit-taking given RSI>80 at exceeded target. New stop raised to $550 (trailing up from $395).

Price $725.38 just exceeded $720 target. RSI=86 is extremely overbought. BUT thesis has materially strengthened: Wells Fargo raised PT to $825, Cantor to $815. GLP-1 TAM expanding. Per Lesson 5, raising target to $810. Per Lesson 4, RSI=86 at target triggers partial profit-taking. New stop raised to $600 (from $485).

Price $719.78 approaching $740 target. RSI=74, not yet triggering profit-taking rule (needs >80 at target). New CFO hire is neutral — management transition risk is low at Costco given strong operational culture. Thesis intact. Monitor for RSI expansion as it nears target.

reduce_positions.reason: Per Lessons 4+5: price exceeded original $620 target at $700.99 with RSI=82. Thesis remains strong so raising target to $800, but taking ~half off the table. Sell 7 of 14 shares. Keep remaining shares for the strengthened AI infrastructure thesis with new target $800 and raised stop $550.

Per Lessons 4+5: price exceeded $720 target at $725.38 with RSI=86 (extremely overbought). Analyst upgrades to $815-825 confirm thesis strengthening, so raising target to $810. Sell 5 of 11 shares. Keep remaining shares with new target $810 and raised stop $600.

lessons: When multiple sell-side analysts raise price targets in the same week on a position already at target, it confirms thesis strengthening (Lesson 5 applies) — but don't let upgraded targets override the RSI discipline (Lesson 4). Execute partial profits on the overbought signal, then ride the remainder with the higher target. The two lessons work together, not against each other.
response.weekly_summary: Week of Feb 5-7: Quiet macro environment. Both NVDA (+45% from entry) and LLY (+22%) exceeded original targets with overbought RSI readings. Taking partial profits on both per established discipline while raising targets (NVDA to $800, LLY to $810) given strengthened theses — AI capex acceleration and multiple analyst upgrades on LLY. COST approaching target at $719 but RSI not yet triggering. No new positions opened; emerging opportunities (F, TENB, etc.) don't offer compelling theme alignment at current valuations. Portfolio remains conservatively positioned with ~84% cash.

response.world_assessment: Quiet macro week with no major policy shifts. AI narrative continues to strengthen — analysts projecting AI market to $400B, Mizuho raised NVDA target to $825, and multiple hyperscalers confirming AI capex acceleration. GLP-1 space remains hot with LLY as clear leader (Kerrisdale shorting competitor Altimmune validates LLY's dominant position). No significant headwinds to our core theses. The main signal this week is technical: both NVDA (RSI 84) and LLY (RSI 88) are deeply overbought at or above their original targets, triggering our profit-taking discipline.

thesis_updates.notes: Thesis materially stronger. AI market projections expanding to $400B (analysts cite NVDA, AMD, AVGO as biggest beneficiaries). Mizuho raised PT to $825. WallStreetBets buzz and institutional whale activity confirm momentum. Price $739 is 19% above original $620 target — per Lesson 5, raising target to $825 to reflect strengthened thesis. However, RSI=84 triggers Lesson 4/6: take partial profits now, ride remainder to new target.

Thesis intact and strengthening. Kerrisdale shorting competitor Altimmune ('obvious dead end') reinforces LLY's dominant GLP-1 position. 5-year return of 6x validates long-term thesis. Price $757 is 5% above $720 target with RSI=88 (extremely overbought). Per Lesson 4, must take partial profits. Raising target to $850 given expanding obesity drug TAM and pipeline catalysts.

Thesis intact. Price $722 approaching $740 target but RSI=69 (not yet overbought) and MACD bearish suggests consolidation rather than breakout. No action needed — will monitor for either a push through target with high RSI (triggering partial profit-take) or a pullback entry to add.

reduce_positions.reason: RSI=84 and price ($739) well above original $620 target. Per Lessons 4/5/6: thesis has strengthened (raising target to $825) but overbought technicals demand partial profit-taking. Trimming ~30% of position. Will hold remainder for revised $825 target with stop raised to $580.

RSI=88 and price ($757) above original $720 target. Per Lessons 4/5: thesis strengthened (raising target to $850) but extreme overbought reading demands partial profit-taking. Trimming ~30% of position. Will hold remainder for revised $850 target with stop raised to $620.

lessons: When trimming overbought winners, raise the stop on the remaining position to lock in gains — don't let a winning trade turn into a loss. New stop should be at or above the original entry price, ideally near a technical support level. This protects profits while allowing the strengthened thesis to play out.
response.weekly_summary: Week of Feb 9-14: AI infrastructure narrative continued to accelerate with analysts projecting $400B AI market and Mizuho raising NVDA target to $825. Both NVDA (RSI 84, +53% from entry) and LLY (RSI 88, +28% from entry) triggered overbought profit-taking discipline — trimmed ~30% of each while raising targets to reflect strengthened theses (NVDA to $825, LLY to $850). COST holding steady near target with manageable RSI. No new positions opened — emerging opportunities (CART, LYFT, TSLA) lacked compelling thesis alignment. Portfolio remains conservatively positioned at ~89% cash with quality holdings in AI infrastructure, GLP-1 healthcare, and defensive retail.

response.world_assessment: NVIDIA's Q4 earnings dominated the week — Data Center revenue of $18.4B (+409% YoY) confirms the AI infrastructure buildout is accelerating far beyond expectations. CEO Jensen Huang says they cannot keep up with demand in the short term. However, headwinds are emerging: antitrust inquiries from France, EU, UK, and China, plus significant China sales declines due to US export restrictions. Stock traded lower post-earnings despite the blowout results, suggesting the bar is now extremely high. Broader market is extended with most quality names showing RSI in the 70s — no compelling entry points this week. Portfolio remains 92% cash, which is patient but increasingly underinvested given thesis confirmation.

thesis_updates.notes: Thesis massively confirmed. Q4 Data Center revenue $18.4B (+409% YoY), Gaming +56%, Pro Viz +105%. CEO says demand exceeds supply. However, new risks: antitrust probes from 4 jurisdictions, China sales declining significantly due to export controls, no licenses to ship restricted chips (A800/H800). Stock sold off post-earnings despite blowout — bar is now sky-high. Per Lesson 5, raising target from $620 to $950 given thesis strengthening. RSI=74 with bearish MACD and near upper BB — not adding here. Watch for post-earnings pullback to add size given we're very underweight for our conviction level.

Price at $769.64, well above original $720 target. No negative news this week. GLP-1 thesis remains intact and strengthening. Per Lesson 5, raising target to $880 — the obesity TAM continues to expand and LLY has best-in-class data. RSI=73 with bullish MACD is constructive. Not yet triggering Lesson 4 partial profit (need RSI > 80 at target), but monitoring closely.

Price at $734.40, within 1% of $740 target. RSI=73 with bearish MACD is concerning — momentum is fading as we approach target. Per Lesson 4, if RSI exceeds 75 at target, take partial profits. MACD bearish divergence at the target zone suggests the easy money has been made. Keeping target at $740 — unlike NVDA/LLY, no material thesis strengthening to justify raising it. Preparing to trim if RSI pushes above 75.

lessons: When NVDA reports earnings that blow away estimates but the stock still sells off, it signals that expectations have gotten ahead of even exceptional results. In future quarters, consider trimming 10-20% of position ahead of earnings when RSI > 70 AND the stock has rallied 30%+ into the print. You can always re-enter on the post-earnings dip. The sell-the-news pattern is strongest when positioning and sentiment are already euphoric.
response.weekly_summary: Week of Feb 17-22: NVDA Q4 earnings confirmed AI thesis with 409% YoY data center growth, but stock sold off post-report despite blowout numbers — expectations now priced extremely high. Raised NVDA target to $950, LLY target to $880 per Lesson 5 (thesis strengthening). COST approaching $740 target with bearish MACD — preparing to trim. Portfolio remains 92% cash with no new entries — all watchlist names showing RSIs in the 70s with no attractive entry points. Patience warranted; waiting for pullbacks to deploy capital. New risk factor: multi-jurisdiction antitrust scrutiny on NVDA's GPU allocation practices.

response.world_assessment: Quiet macro week heading into month-end. Key portfolio dynamics: all three positions have now exceeded their original price targets. NVDA continues to benefit from AI infrastructure narrative — BofA reiterates data center dominance, market expert pushes back on bubble comparisons noting NVDA is cheaper on forward earnings than 26 months ago. LLY faces emerging competitive noise from Viking Therapeutics' weight loss trial data that outperformed both NVO and LLY studies, but FDA's crackdown on compounded/fake GLP-1 drugs is a positive for branded manufacturers. COST quietly hit its $740 target on continued consumer trade-down dynamics. No significant macro dislocations — market grinding higher with RSI elevated across our holdings.

thesis_updates.notes: At $791, now 27% above original $620 target. Thesis materially strengthened — BofA confirms data center dominance, valuation still reasonable on forward earnings. Per Lesson 5, raising target to $900. RSI at 72, approaching but not yet at the 80 trim trigger (Lesson 4). Per Lesson 8, should watch for earnings positioning — if RSI climbs above 75 heading into next report, consider pre-earnings trim. Hold full position for now.

At $753, above $720 target. Viking Therapeutics posted competitive weight loss data — worth monitoring but LLY has massive manufacturing and distribution moat that Viking lacks. FDA crackdown on compounded GLP-1s is net positive for branded LLY products. Indian market expansion adds TAM. RSI at 64, not overbought. Per Lesson 5, raising target to $850. MACD bearish is a near-term caution flag but not thesis-breaking.

At $743.89, essentially at $740 target. RSI=72, elevated but below 80 trim trigger. Consumer value thesis intact — no signs of membership fatigue. Per Lesson 5, raising target to $800 given continued market share gains and defensive positioning. Watch RSI — if it pushes above 80 at these levels, take partial profits per Lesson 4.

lessons: When a competitor reports strong clinical trial data against one of our pharma holdings (e.g., Viking vs LLY in GLP-1), don't panic-sell — instead assess whether the competitor has the manufacturing scale, regulatory approvals, and distribution to actually capture share within our investment horizon. Early-stage biotech trial data rarely translates to near-term market share loss for established players. Flag for thesis review only if competitor reaches Phase 3 with superior data AND has credible manufacturing plans.
response.weekly_summary: Week of Feb 24-29: All three positions now trading above original targets. Raised targets on all — NVDA to $900 (AI thesis strengthening, valuation still reasonable per BofA), LLY to $850 (FDA GLP-1 crackdown positive, Viking competitive data not yet material threat), COST to $800 (consumer value thesis intact). Portfolio remains conservatively positioned at 92% cash with total invested value of ~$9K against $104K portfolio. No compelling new entries this week — emerging opportunities (SRI, LPSN, WHD, FIGS, EFXT) don't align with our themes. Watching RSI levels on NVDA and COST as both at 72 and approaching potential trim territory per Lessons 4-7.

response.world_assessment: Relatively quiet macro week. Key dynamics: (1) Powell testimony ahead — market on hopium for rate cuts, Lesson 3 flags this as volatility catalyst. (2) NYCB rescue with $1B equity raise and Mnuchin joining board signals regional banking stress persists but is being managed. (3) Bitcoin hitting new highs reflecting risk-on sentiment. (4) AI demand narrative continues strengthening — AWS exec confirms NVDA chip demand stays high, Super Micro earnings skyrocketing. (5) Election dynamics shifting with Biden retaking lead in polls. Overall risk-on environment with stretched technicals across our portfolio.

thesis_updates.notes: Thesis materially stronger. AWS exec publicly confirming demand for NVDA chips stays high. Super Micro earnings skyrocketing on AI demand validates the infrastructure buildout thesis. Stock at $926 vs $620 target — applying Lesson 5 to raise target to $1,050. However, RSI=84 triggers Lesson 4 — must take partial profits. Applying Lesson 6: analyst upgrades + overbought = trim and ride remainder with higher target.

Thesis intact and strengthening. News confirms 'escalating demand for obesity drugs creates time-sensitive race' for LLY and Novo. Whale activity detected in health care stocks. MACD bearish is a near-term caution flag but RSI=66 is healthy — no action needed. Price at $780 vs $720 target — raising target to $880 per Lesson 5 given expanding TAM confirmation. Hold full position.

Thesis intact. Technical momentum building ahead of Q2 earnings. Membership fee increase rumor is bullish for the recurring revenue thesis. However, RSI=85 and price $785 vs $740 target triggers Lesson 4 — take partial profits. Raising target to $830 per Lesson 5 given potential membership fee catalyst, but must respect overbought discipline.

reduce_positions.reason: RSI=84 with price ($926) 53% above original $620 target. Lessons 4+6+7 all apply: take partial profits, raise stop on remainder. Trimming ~50% of position (7 shares). Raising target to $1,050 and stop to $750 (above entry, per Lesson 7) on remaining shares.

RSI=85 with price ($785) above $740 target. Lesson 4 triggered. Trimming ~40% ahead of earnings — if earnings are strong, can re-add on any post-report consolidation. Raising target to $830 and stop to $700 (above entry, per Lesson 7) on remaining shares.

lessons: When 92% in cash with all three positions overbought (RSI > 80 on two of three), the priority is harvesting gains to redeploy — not adding new positions. Patient capital means being willing to sit in cash and wait for better entries. Don't feel pressure to deploy the 92% cash balance just because it's large; the right entries will come on pullbacks.
response.weekly_summary: Week of Mar 2-7, 2024: Risk-on environment with AI demand narrative strengthening (AWS confirms NVDA chip demand, SMCI earnings surge). Trimming NVDA and COST on overbought RSI signals per established lessons — both well past original targets. Raising targets on all three positions as theses have materially strengthened. LLY held at full size with healthy RSI. Portfolio remains very cash-heavy (92%) providing ample dry powder for pullback entries. Watching Powell testimony as potential volatility catalyst.

response.world_assessment: Quiet macro week with no major headline shifts. The most significant development is LLY's partnership with Amazon Pharmacy to deliver Zepbound and other prescriptions through LillyDirect — this meaningfully expands LLY's direct-to-consumer distribution moat. Counterfeit weight-loss drug crime jumping 50% underscores massive unmet demand for GLP-1s. Oprah's upcoming Ozempic TV special will drive further mainstream awareness. NVDA continues to get positive sentiment (Cramer highlight) and technicals are bullish. No macro disruptions to our core themes — AI buildout and GLP-1 adoption continue unabated.

thesis_updates.notes: Thesis materially strengthened. Amazon Pharmacy partnership for Zepbound/LillyDirect delivery is a major distribution catalyst — removes friction for patients and bypasses traditional pharmacy bottlenecks. Counterfeit drug surge confirms explosive demand. Oprah TV special incoming as awareness catalyst. Price at $760.73 has exceeded original $720 target. Per Lesson 5, raising target to $850 given strengthened thesis. RSI at 56 is healthy — no trim signal per Lesson 4.

Thesis intact. Approaching $740 target at $731.95. RSI at 50 is neutral — no overbought signal. FTC Kroger-Albertsons trial set for August could be a tailwind if the merger is blocked (less competition). Options flow being watched by smart money. Hold and monitor. Will reassess at target if RSI stays moderate.
Was stopped out previously but AI infrastructure thesis is stronger than ever. Price now at $879 with bullish MACD and RSI at 63. Considering re-entry with updated levels.

new_positions.thesis: Re-entering NVDA after stop-out. The AI infrastructure buildout thesis is stronger than when we first entered — hyperscaler capex continues accelerating, CUDA ecosystem lock-in deepens, and data center revenue is in a multi-quarter acceleration. The stock has moved significantly higher, confirming the thesis was correct even though we were stopped out. At $879, NVDA trades at a premium but the TAM expansion (inference scaling, sovereign AI, enterprise adoption) justifies re-entry. Blackwell architecture launch provides next catalyst cycle.
new_positions.invalidation: Data center revenue growth decelerates below 50% YoY, or a credible CUDA alternative gains meaningful enterprise adoption, or hyperscalers materially cut AI capex plans
new_positions.timing_note: RSI at 63 is healthy, MACD bullish, above SMA50. Not chasing — entering on confirmed trend with room to run.

lessons: When a position gets stopped out but the underlying thesis proves correct (NVDA stopped at $395, now at $879), the stop was too tight relative to the thesis timeframe. For high-conviction AI infrastructure plays with 3-6 month horizons, consider whether the catastrophic stop (18%) is sufficient or if the automatic stop needs a wider berth during volatile periods. The lesson is not to remove stops — it's to ensure stop placement matches thesis conviction and volatility profile. Re-entering at a higher price is acceptable when the thesis is validated.
response.weekly_summary: Week of Mar 9-14: Quiet macro backdrop allowed us to focus on thesis validation. LLY thesis materially strengthened by Amazon Pharmacy partnership for Zepbound delivery — raised target from $720 to $850. COST steady approaching $740 target with neutral RSI. Re-entering NVDA at $879 after previous stop-out — AI infrastructure thesis validated by continued hyperscaler spending. Portfolio moving from 95.6% cash to ~89% cash, deliberately patient deployment. Two positions in profit (LLY +28%, COST +12%), adding back NVDA as third position.

response.world_assessment: Quiet macro week with no major catalysts. Markets grinding higher. Fed policy remains data-dependent. The 2024 election narrative is building but too early to position around. Key portfolio development: LLY's Zepbound overtaking Wegovy in US prescriptions is a significant competitive milestone. NVDA continues to consolidate in a bull flag with multiple analyst notes confirming AI capex cycle. Both LLY and COST have exceeded their original price targets.

thesis_updates.notes: Thesis materially strengthened. Zepbound overtaking Wegovy in US prescriptions confirms LLY's best-in-class positioning in obesity. Stock at $770 has exceeded $720 target. Per Lesson 5, raising target to $880 given expanding market share and GLP-1 TAM growth. RSI 58 is healthy — no need to trim yet. Will apply Lesson 4 if RSI exceeds 80 at new target.

Stock at $742 has reached $740 target. RSI 55 is neutral, MACD bearish suggests near-term softness possible. Thesis intact — consumer trade-down trend continues. Raising target modestly to $790 per Lesson 5, as the value retail thesis hasn't weakened. No trim signal since RSI is well below 80.

Consolidating at $914 in bull flag pattern. Multiple analyst notes confirm AI demand pull driving 2024 datacenter capex. RSI 68 approaching overbought but not there yet. MACD bearish suggests short-term consolidation, which is healthy after the run from re-entry at $879. Thesis intact, target $1050 unchanged.

response.weekly_summary: Week of Mar 16-21: Quiet consolidation week. LLY reached a significant milestone with Zepbound overtaking Wegovy in US prescriptions, validating the GLP-1 leadership thesis — raised target from $720 to $880. COST also hit its original $740 target with thesis intact, raised to $790. NVDA consolidating in bull flag near $914 with continued analyst support for AI capex thesis. Portfolio up nicely with all three positions above entry. Remaining 89.8% cash with no compelling new entries this week — emerging opportunity tickers (HOFV, NSYS, FGI, DKNG, PARA) don't align with our themes. Patience remains the strategy; waiting for quality pullback entries in theme-aligned names.

response.world_assessment: Quiet week macro-wise. Yen hitting 34-year lows against the dollar signals continued USD strength and potential BOJ intervention risk — relevant for multinationals like LLY with Japan exposure. Election polling tightening between Trump and Biden introduces policy uncertainty but no actionable trades yet. NVDA trading near all-time highs with whale options activity and Jim Cramer noting the pullback is market broadening, not a selloff — consistent with our thesis. TSMC 3nm demand from Apple/Intel/AMD confirms the AI infrastructure buildout is broadening beyond just NVDA. No major macro shocks this week.

thesis_updates.notes: Trading at $903.56 vs $879.44 entry (+2.7%). RSI 60 is healthy — not overbought. MACD bearish suggests near-term consolidation which is fine for our 3-6 month horizon. Whale options activity and TSMC 3nm demand confirm AI infrastructure buildout continues. Cramer's comment about market broadening (not selloff) aligns with our view. Thesis strengthening. Hold.

LLY has blown past original $720 target at $778 (+31.4% from entry) — target raised to $900 given strengthening GLP-1 thesis and moderate RSI. COST approaching $740 target at $733 (+12.6%). No new positions opened — emerging opportunities (INTT, CC, SLE) don't align with our themes. Sitting at 89.8% cash per Lesson 10 discipline, waiting for pullback entries in quality names.
